title: Best Time or WINNING? The Brutal Truth Elite Swimmers Won't Admit 🔥
description: Dax & Elvis go head-to-head: Is dropping a PB the real flex... or is it making your opponents QUIT and fear you in the pool? We break down why wins are greater than times at college level and beyon...
transcript:
Dax & Elvis go head-to-head: Is dropping a PB the real flex... or is it making your opponents QUIT and fear you in the pool? We break down why wins are greater than times at college level and beyond, killer mentality, and why swimming needs more "struggle for superiority." Blackstroke Nation, drop your take—who's right? 👇 Hit like/subscribe for more real talk.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: What's Wrong with Kieran Smith? No PB's in 5 Years. Is it time to leave Gainesville?
description: Kieran Smith is stacking World Championship teams and medals... but no best times in forever and zero buzz. Dax & Elvis ask the tough question: Is it time to leave Gainesville? We compare his yarda...
transcript:
Kieran Smith is stacking World Championship teams and medals... but no best times in forever and zero buzz. Dax & Elvis ask the tough question: Is it time to leave Gainesville? We compare his yardage overload to past mistakes, why high-volume distance training can backfire, and why elite freestylers belong at Cal for power/technique fixes. Marketing fail or deeper issue? Mental baseline reset after big success? Brutal honesty.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd.

title: Michael Andrew: "I'm DONE with the 200 IM" – Why He's Dropping It for Good!
description: Sprint king Michael Andrew opens up about finally walking away from the 200 Individual Medley after years of battling its demands, explaining how he's shifting full focus to his powerhouse 50s and ...
transcript:
Sprint king Michael Andrew opens up about finally walking away from the 200 Individual Medley after years of battling its demands, explaining how he's shifting full focus to his powerhouse 50s and 100s events (freestyle, breast, fly) to maximize his potential and avoid the endurance grind that never quite clicked for his USRPT style—real talk on why this move makes sense post-2024 Trials disappointment and as he rebuilds toward LA 2028. Is this the smart pivot or a missed opportunity? Drop your thoughts below! 🔥 #MichaelAndrew #Swimming #200IM #SprintSwim

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: 13-Year-Old Superboy Swims 4KM in Ocean to Save Family – The Killer Mentality We Need 🦸‍♂️
description: Austin Appleby (13) ditches life jacket, battles cold waves, swims miles to get help—pure intolerance for losing. Dax reframes it: this is the "burn the ships" mindset missing from modern swimming....
transcript:
Austin Appleby (13) ditches life jacket, battles cold waves, swims miles to get help—pure intolerance for losing. Dax reframes it: this is the "burn the ships" mindset missing from modern swimming. Hate losing more than you love winning? We connect it to elite athletes tapping survival mode. Hero story + deep mindset talk. Legend. #SwimHero #KillerMindset #NightSwim

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Women Are Breaking World Records at a Rate Men Can’t Match - Here’s Why
description:
Women are stacking multiple world records - and the men aren’t.
In this Night Swim clip, we dive into why female swimmers currently dominate the world-record leaderboard, why the numbers look wild ...
transcript:
Women are stacking multiple world records - and the men aren’t.
In this Night Swim clip, we dive into why female swimmers currently dominate the world-record leaderboard, why the numbers look wild on paper, and why this isn’t as simple as “women are better now.”

Dax & Elvis talk:
Short course vs long course (aka record inflation season)
The American college system and strength training cheat codes
Why the data looks shocking until you actually read it
Who’s benefiting, who’s getting cooked, and why nobody wants to say it out loud

It's a late-night swimming conversation with no lifeguards on deck and zero respect for bad stats.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Katie Ledecky Drops 2nd FASTEST 1500 EVER... But We're Getting BORED? (Wild Fix Idea!)
description: Katie Ledecky just swam 15:23.21—the SECOND fastest 1500 freestyle in HISTORY—at Pro Swim Series Austin! She's owning the top 12 all-time spots, but Dax & Elvis admit: We're starting to take her do...
transcript:
Katie Ledecky just swam 15:23.21—the SECOND fastest 1500 freestyle in HISTORY—at Pro Swim Series Austin! She's owning the top 12 all-time spots, but Dax & Elvis admit: We're starting to take her dominance for granted like prime Jordan. Is she TOO good for the sport? Elvis drops a $5,000 bounty challenge: Mid-race tea sip + book read + Night Swim shoutout to save swimming's entertainment value! 😂 Would you watch? Ledecky needs to go villain mode!

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Why Cal Baptist Cut Their Swim Team… And Why Top Swimmers NEED to Post More in 2026
description: Dax & Elvis break down the shocking Cal Baptist swim team cancellation and Jon Reiter's hot take that swimmers aren't posting enough content. Is the "swim influencer" era the key to saving college ...
transcript:
Dax & Elvis break down the shocking Cal Baptist swim team cancellation and Jon Reiter's hot take that swimmers aren't posting enough content. Is the "swim influencer" era the key to saving college programs? We also discuss Gretchen Walsh's failed New Year's resolution to post more and why elite swimmers are leaving money on the table. Night Swim is back with unfiltered takes! 🔥

Subscribe + turn on notifications for daily swimming drama & hot takes →

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Fix Your Starts & Turns NOW or Lose at NCAAs (Dax & Elvis Roast Bad Technique)
description:
Dax & Elvis go OFF on terrible starts, turns, and technique in college swimming — especially after watching conference meets.
They call out Arizona State’s Ilia (Ilya) for painfully slow turns desp...
transcript:
Dax & Elvis go OFF on terrible starts, turns, and technique in college swimming — especially after watching conference meets.
They call out Arizona State’s Ilia (Ilya) for painfully slow turns despite being unstoppable, roast Big 12 teams for the worst execution they've seen, and explain why Tennessee dominates relay starts with low-swing athletic dives (not bunny hops or flopping in).
Plus: Why open turns in fly/breast are the SAME skill (mind blown for many), how Gen Z swimmers lack basic body awareness from no tree climbing/outdoor play, the "Gen Z stare," programming habits during easy 100s, and brutal advice — fix your turns or get exposed at NCAAs.
Unfiltered swim real talk, no SwimSwam recap vibes. Perfect for age-groupers, high schoolers, college swimmers, and coaches prepping for short course season, Texas Age Group Champs, D1 NCAAs, and sectionals.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Josh Liendo Spills: Florida Training, Dressel Exit, SCM WR 100 Fly, Weight Room Secrets & More
description:
The lifeguards are GONE — it's time for real swim talk.

For the FIRST TIME EVER on Night Swim, we welcome not one, not two, but THREE Black swimmers on the mic — a world record in itself 😂. Olympi...
transcript:
The lifeguards are GONE — it's time for real swim talk.

For the FIRST TIME EVER on Night Swim, we welcome not one, not two, but THREE Black swimmers on the mic — a world record in itself 😂. Olympic silver medalist (100 fly Paris 2024), multiple-time NCAA champion, short course WORLD RECORD holder (47.68 100 fly), and Florida Gator legend Josh Liendo joins Dax & Elvis for an unfiltered, no-PR conversation.

We get into:

The real culture at Florida under Anthony Nesty (the OG butterflyer)
What actually happened with Bella Sims drama & team meetings (Josh keeps it 100)
Why Caleb Dressel left Florida & the power of coach-athlete relationships
The rise of Black swimmers — from Jordan Crooks battles to the snowball effect
Taper myths, high yardage vs explosiveness, and when you can get TOO swole in the weight room
Growing up in Trinidad/Canada, representing Black excellence in a mostly white sport
Favorite muscle for the ladies, rom-com vibes, pickup lines (the youth need this game 😂)
Enhanced Games? LA 2028 plans? And why he's staying focused

Plus chain flexing, roasting Hubert Kos's basketball skills, George Bovell beach warm-ups, and way too much laughing after hours.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Elite Mentality: What Separates Champions from Competitors | Night Swim Podcast
description: The difference between good and great isn't talent—it's mentality. Dax and Elvis break down what it really takes to compete at the elite level: the mental toughness, the discipline, the grind that ...
transcript:
The difference between good and great isn't talent—it's mentality. Dax and Elvis break down what it really takes to compete at the elite level: the mental toughness, the discipline, the grind that most swimmers won't talk about.

🏊‍♂️ What elite mentality actually looks like
🧠 How champions think differently
🔥 The brutal truth about competing at the top

When you're standing behind the blocks at NCAAs or Trials, everyone is fast. What separates you is what's between your ears.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who've seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.

New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.

🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd

#swimming #ncaaswimming #olympics #swimtalk #nightswim #elitementality #mentalhealth #sportspsychology

title: Josh Liendo's Heisman Lane Push + Texas Sprint Struggles | Night Swim SEC Recap
description: Unfiltered SEC Championships recap on Night Swim Podcast! Dax Hill (Texas Longhorns legend, NCAA champ) and Elvis Burrows break down the "Texas Invite" that is now the SECs. Josh Liendo dominates w...
transcript:
Unfiltered SEC Championships recap on Night Swim Podcast! Dax Hill (Texas Longhorns legend, NCAA champ) and Elvis Burrows break down the "Texas Invite" that is now the SECs. Josh Liendo dominates with 18.4s leads, 43.0 100 fly, relay monster splits — plus the hilarious lane-line "Heisman" celebration gone wrong (who pushed him back?!). We dive into why Texas still can't sprint (Dax spills on his rejected $2k sprint clinic offer), suit inflation making 39s/19s normal(?), Tennessee's Black sprint takeover with Eva Okaro & co. dropping 45s/20s relay legs, and how building around 50/100 free wins conferences & NCAAs (Cal blueprint). Big-picture strategy, no lifeguards, pure pool-deck real talk.
Hosted by @SwimNerds (Dax & Elvis). Night Swim = behind-the-curtain swimming culture, no SwimSwam results recap here.


🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

description: Olympic swimming legend Ryan Lochte's string of bad luck continues...While competing on "Worst Cook in America," the 12-time Olympic medalist slipped on SPAGHETTI and injured himself badly enough t...
transcript:
Olympic swimming legend Ryan Lochte's string of bad luck continues...While competing on "Worst Cook in America," the 12-time Olympic medalist slipped on SPAGHETTI and injured himself badly enough to be eliminated from the show. We break down:• How this happened (making tacos while spaghetti was on the floor??)• Lochte's bizarre injury history• Why this might be the most "Ryan Lochte" story ever
From the Night Swim Podcast with Elvis & Dax - the realest swim talk on the internet.Subscribe for daily swim content that doesn't sugarcoat anything.
#ryanlochte  #Swimming #Olympics #nightswimpod

No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who've seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd

title: Women's Big Ten Championships – 59 A-Cuts from B & C Finals?!
description: Dax and Elvis react to the insane number of automatic NCAA qualifying times (A-cuts) that came out of the B- and C-finals at the 2026 Women's Big Ten Championships. They discuss what this means for...
transcript:
Dax and Elvis react to the insane number of automatic NCAA qualifying times (A-cuts) that came out of the B- and C-finals at the 2026 Women's Big Ten Championships. They discuss what this means for meet speed, qualification chaos, event caps at NCAAs, and whether the new rules are making conference meets paradoxically slower and faster at the same time.

title: China Pays Swimmers $20K Just to Show Up – USA Swimming Exposed!
description: China is shaking up swimming with MASSIVE payouts at the 2026 China Open in Shenzhen – up to $20,000 appearance fees just for showing up, plus $10K for 1st, $8K for 2nd, $6K for 3rd, flights, and h...
transcript:
China is shaking up swimming with MASSIVE payouts at the 2026 China Open in Shenzhen – up to $20,000 appearance fees just for showing up, plus $10K for 1st, $8K for 2nd, $6K for 3rd, flights, and hotels covered for top international stars!

In this episode of Night Swim Podcast, we break down why this meet is a game-changer: elite athletes getting real money, fast exciting racing with only the best, fan experiences, autographs, and star power – while USA Swimming sticks to tiny $2K prizes that barely cover travel.
We rant about:

How USA Swimming & World Aquatics are greedy or incompetent
Why they never capitalized on Phelps-level crowds or pushed stars like Dressel properly
The boring structure of most meets vs. China's smart, short, high-value format
Business lessons: spend money to make money, pay for star power, give fans the experience they want
Marketing fails, "funnest sport" nonsense, and why outsiders have to fix this sport

What do YOU think? Is China saving swimming or just exposing how broken the system is? Drop your take in the comments! 🔥

Subscribe for daily swimming rants, breakdowns, and real talk. Hit the bell 🔔 so you never miss an episode. Like & comment to help push this to more swimmers!

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Grousset SMASHES Dressel's US Open Record & Vows to BREAK His 100 Fly WORLD RECORD!
description: Maxime Grousset is cooking! He smashed Caeleb Dressel's US Open record in the 50 fly semis (22.81!) and already beat him in the 100 fly at the 2026 Pro Swim Series Austin. Dax & Elvis go full send:...
transcript:
Maxime Grousset is cooking! He smashed Caeleb Dressel's US Open record in the 50 fly semis (22.81!) and already beat him in the 100 fly at the 2026 Pro Swim Series Austin. Dax & Elvis go full send: Is this the end of Dressel's fly dominance? We roast the "walking into his house" energy, Dressel's bronze in the 50 fly, his confident post-race interview shift, and why his freestyle technique might be catching up to him. Fans are still ride-or-die—delusional or loyal? 😂

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Michael Andrew: Why I Left ASU, Returned to Dad's USRPT, & My LA 2028 Plan
description:
Michael Andrew joins Dax & Elvis on Night Swim for his most open interview yet.
The Olympic gold medalist opens up about:
• Leaving Arizona State University after one year and returning to train fu...
transcript:
Michael Andrew joins Dax & Elvis on Night Swim for his most open interview yet.
The Olympic gold medalist opens up about:
• Leaving Arizona State University after one year and returning to train full-time in Hawaii with his dad (Peter Andrew) using USRPT roots
• Emotional low after missing Paris 2024, family challenges, and rebuilding mentally
• LA 2028 goals: 50s events (breast, fly, free), 100 breast, possible 100 fly + relays
• Thoughts on Ilya joining Team USA, racing Shane Casas & the new sprint landscape
• The vaccine controversy, death threats, media hits, and how he built unbreakable mental armor
• Why he skipped the 200 IM forever after Tokyo scars + memes
• Training secrets: KAATSU blood flow restriction, Destro buckets, ASP dryland with Sean Santos, and why specificity is greater than volume
• Pro swimming reality: ISL experience (no owed money), why it failed, future pro teams, CSL thoughts
• Life goals post-2028: Surfing, jet skis, farm life in Hawaii, raising "island babies"
Raw, real talk from one of swimming's most polarizing (and resilient) stars. Lifeguards are gone — anything goes.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming 

00:01 — Night Swim Welcomes Olympic Gold Medalist Michael Andrew (Intro)
0:17 — Elvis checks in + Santorini hoodie / Greece trip
0:59 — “Swim families don’t get vacations” + Dax’s first vacation story
1:40 — Comment section chaos: “you can’t reach behind your back”
2:07 — Elvis: “No more free advice” + funnel talk
3:06 — Bringing on the guest: Michael Andrew joins the pod
3:51 — Throwbacks: Derby / Chesapeake Pro-Am / jungle gym interview
4:53 — “Not much has changed… beard + bigger muscles”
5:46 — Michael Andrew’s Ambitious LA 2028 Olympic Goals (event focus + 50s return)
8:19 — One-stroke life? Why Michael likes versatility
9:37 — From 200 IM Scars to Ilya Malynov’s US Team Impact (Tokyo trauma + Ilya talk)
13:50 — Why Michael Andrew Left ASU and Returned to USRPT (post-trials reset + environment)
20:00 — Inside ASP Dryland and the Future of Pro Swim Teams (Caleb/Santos + pro teams)
23:53 — Michael Andrew’s Take on ISL and the CSL League (money owed, why ISL could work)
25:58 — “Hard to make swimming sexy” (betting/alcohol/entertainment angle)
27:37 — Enhanced Games: why he’s out + “farm in Hawaii” dream
29:56 — Building Mental Armor After Vaccine Controversy & Hate (media, criticism, lessons)
42:41 — The Untold Story of Michael Andrew’s Adidas Deal (Elvis negotiation story)
45:13 — Elvis suit ripped story + Adidas “take it down” moment
46:06 — Michael’s Adidas gun photo story (brand panic)
47:10 — Michael Andrew on USA Swimming, Kevin & Athlete Compensation (stipends, content)
52:43 — “Start an internet beef” + Logan Paul ad-play talk
55:03 — Why Swimmers Need to Embrace Content Creation & Branding
56:49 — Katsu training: BFR explanation + sprint benefits
59:11 — Destro buckets + resistance without ruining stroke
1:12:19 — Unpacking Michael Andrew’s Unique Swimming Technique (asymmetry + body line debate)
1:25:03 — Michael Andrew’s Vision for Life After LA 2028 (Hawaii hang + future plans)
1:27:19 — Ranch guy or compound guy? (warehouse, toys, eggs, land)
1:29:10 — Michael Andrew’s Final Thoughts & Night Swim Outro (plugs + wrap)

title: Sprint Technique Secret: You NEED Long Stroke Efficiency First (Controversial Take)
description: Everyone says grind yards to build your aerobic base before sprinting… but is that garbage? Dax and Elvis argue the REAL foundation for elite sprinting is learning long, efficient long-course strok...
transcript:
Everyone says grind yards to build your aerobic base before sprinting… but is that garbage? Dax and Elvis argue the REAL foundation for elite sprinting is learning long, efficient long-course strokes first. Short-course power comes easy after that - but going the other way? Good luck. This controversial take will piss off half the coaches in America. No lifeguards on duty.
Comment: Did you learn meters or yards first? How'd it affect your speed?

Smash that subscribe - we're just getting started in 2026.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd.

title: $2K Bounty on Josh Liendo: Will He Do a Touchdown Dance & Shout Out Night Swim?!
description: Dax & Elvis throw down a $2,000 challenge to star swimmer Josh Liendo (Florida Gators / Canada): Do an epic touchdown dance (or sick celebration) after winning at NCAAs + shout out "Night Swim Podc...
transcript:
Dax & Elvis throw down a $2,000 challenge to star swimmer Josh Liendo (Florida Gators / Canada): Do an epic touchdown dance (or sick celebration) after winning at NCAAs + shout out "Night Swim Podcast" in the post-race interview = cash money!

Josh breaks it down: Why celebrations sometimes go out the window after a big swim, iconic team cellys (like Florida's row the boat), Chad Johnson-inspired golf swings on the lane line, backflip risks, cannonball DQs, relay group ideas (Ginyu Force pose?), and why he's tempted to go big in his senior year.

Will he lock it in? Will the whole team Sharpie "Night Swim Podcast" on their chests? Hilarious, hype convo about bringing more fun and personality to college swimming.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. title: Taper Is a MYTH in 2026? Why Swimmers Go 18-Mid Year-Round Now – Dax vs Elvis Debate
description: Is the magical "taper unicorn" dead? Dax (Eddie Reese purist) defends the psychological edge of big end-of-season rest, while Elvis argues modern training + recovery cycles mean fast swimming all y...
transcript:
Is the magical "taper unicorn" dead? Dax (Eddie Reese purist) defends the psychological edge of big end-of-season rest, while Elvis argues modern training + recovery cycles mean fast swimming all year (Nathan Adrian style). Heated debate on over/under tapering, adaptation tracking, and why today's swimmers are faster in October than ever. Must-watch for coaches & swimmers!

New episode daily – subscribe now!

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: $1,000 Relay Celebration?! Josh Liendo’s Night Swim Podcast Challenge
description:
What happens if the relay wins… and the celebration gets PERSONAL?

In this electric Night Swim moment, the boys joke about turning a relay victory into a $1,000 double-or-nothing celebration chall...
transcript:
What happens if the relay wins… and the celebration gets PERSONAL?

In this electric Night Swim moment, the boys joke about turning a relay victory into a $1,000 double-or-nothing celebration challenge — with one catch:

“First we gotta win. We gotta lock in first.”

From Sharpie-ing “Night Swim Podcast” across their chest to hyping up the squad before stepping on the blocks, this is the kind of relay energy that makes swimming FUN again.

#NightSwim #JoshLiendo #Swimming #RelaySelly #SwimPodcast #SwimCulture

title: 57% of Coaches Get THREATENED by Parents – SafeSport Nightmare Exposed 😡
description: SwimSwam drops bombs: over half of coaches face parent threats. We unpack why—lack of rep, no clear standards, kids weaponizing "bullying." Dax & Elvis on standing on business, building fear/respec...
transcript:
SwimSwam drops bombs: over half of coaches face parent threats. We unpack why—lack of rep, no clear standards, kids weaponizing "bullying." Dax & Elvis on standing on business, building fear/respect, and why some coaches get punked. Coaches & parents: sound off in comments! This is the movement. #SafeSport #SwimCoaching #NightSwim

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: NCAA Swimming Is Ruining Itself. And Brian Schrader Just Said It.
description:
Ohio State associate head coach Brian Schrader is speaking out against the NCAA’s recent championship changes — and he’s not holding back.

From eliminating B finals to altering qualification stand...
transcript:
Ohio State associate head coach Brian Schrader is speaking out against the NCAA’s recent championship changes — and he’s not holding back.

From eliminating B finals to altering qualification standards, Schrader argues that these updates hurt elite competition and leave faster swimmers at home.

Are these changes about fairness… or about making swimming more “marketable”?

In this episode of Night Swim, we break down what Schrader said — and what it means for the future of NCAA swimming.

👇 Let us know your take in the comments.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Sodium Bicarbonate in Swimming – Fad or Legit Performance Booster?
description: Deep dive into sodium bicarbonate (baking soda) supplementation as a buffering agent against lactic acid, its risks (GI distress), small potential gains (1-2%), comparison to past fads like beet ju...
transcript:
Deep dive into sodium bicarbonate (baking soda) supplementation as a buffering agent against lactic acid, its risks (GI distress), small potential gains (1-2%), comparison to past fads like beet juice and creatine, and whether the reward outweighs the "volcano" side effects.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Men's ACC Champs: Freshman Phenom Max Williamson + Why UVA Men Are Struggling Under Todd DeSorbo
description: Dax and Elvis break down the 2026 Men's ACC Swimming Championships with zero lifeguards and full unfiltered takes. Freshman standout Max Williamson (UVA) drops bombs in the 200 IM (silver medal, sc...
transcript:
Dax and Elvis break down the 2026 Men's ACC Swimming Championships with zero lifeguards and full unfiltered takes. Freshman standout Max Williamson (UVA) drops bombs in the 200 IM (silver medal, school record vibes) and hits A-cuts to lock in NCAA spots—high school 41.0 sprinter turning heads. They dive deep into whether Todd DeSorbo's style is clicking on the men's side (UVA men finish 7th at 577.5 while women dominate), the masculine vs. feminine coaching debate, and why some programs favor one gender. Shoutouts to Kai Winkler (NC State tall-boy sprinter stepping up), Jeremy Kelly (Dax's old Zones kid grinding through transfers), and Stanford's close 1076 vs. Cal's winning 1154 (fluke or revival?). Classic Night Swim: behind-the-curtain drama, no boring results recaps.
Subscribe for more raw swimming talk—hit the bell!

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Erisman Siblings Are the NEW Swim Royalty! Cal Freshman Ryan Erisman is HIM
description: The Erisman family is taking over! Ryan Erisman (Cal) upset big names like Carson Foster & Luke Hobson for gold in the 200 fly/200 free at Pro Swim Series, while sister Rylee is upsetting vets like...
transcript:
The Erisman family is taking over! Ryan Erisman (Cal) upset big names like Carson Foster & Luke Hobson for gold in the 200 fly/200 free at Pro Swim Series, while sister Rylee is upsetting vets like Simone Manuel. Dax & Elvis discuss: Is this the next big swim family (like Walshes/Fosters)? Why Cal (David Durden/Marsh influence) is fixing technique and unlocking power. Sibling Olympians incoming? Shoutout to the parents! 🏆

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Ryan Lochte Just Sold 3 Olympic Gold Medals for $385K… We Were Right Again 😳
description: Ryan Lochte auctions three Olympic relay gold medals for nearly $400K amid divorce, IRS issues, and legal battles. Dax & Elvis called this months ago and stand by their take: empathy for Lochte whi...
transcript:
Ryan Lochte auctions three Olympic relay gold medals for nearly $400K amid divorce, IRS issues, and legal battles. Dax & Elvis called this months ago and stand by their take: empathy for Lochte while questioning the optics. Full breakdown + why relay medals always go cheaper. Legendary swimmer going through it – thoughts?

Daily episodes – don't miss out!

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: When Dax & Elvis Have NO TOPIC... Things Get WILD 😂 | Night Swim
description:
What happens when Night Swim runs out of topics? Absolute chaos.

Dax and Elvis go off-script and the results are hilarious. This is what happens when two elite swimmers have nothing planned and ju...
transcript:
What happens when Night Swim runs out of topics? Absolute chaos.

Dax and Elvis go off-script and the results are hilarious. This is what happens when two elite swimmers have nothing planned and just start talking.

🎯 In this episode:
• No agenda, no filter, just vibes
• Random swim takes and hot opinions
• Why this might be our most unhinged episode yet

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who've seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.

New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd

#NightSwimPod #Swimming #Swimnerd

title: DITCH Morning Practice?! 🌅 The CONTROVERSIAL Take Every Swimmer Needs to Hear
description:
Is morning practice actually helping you... or holding you back?

Dax and Elvis drop a controversial take that's going to trigger every coach who loves 5 AM wake-ups. But hear them out — the scienc...
transcript:
Is morning practice actually helping you... or holding you back?

Dax and Elvis drop a controversial take that's going to trigger every coach who loves 5 AM wake-ups. But hear them out — the science might be on their side.

🎯 What we cover:
• The case against morning practice
• Sleep science and athletic performance
• Why some elite programs are changing their approach
• When morning practice actually makes sense

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who've seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.

New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd

#NightSwimPod #Swimming #Swimnerd

title: We HATE the Olympic 50m Stroke Qualification Rules
description:
Dax and Elvis share Cam McEvoy's frustration and displeasure with the convoluted Olympic qualification rules for the 50m stroke events (50 Fly, 50 Back, 50 Breast).
They break down why the 50s of s...
transcript:
Dax and Elvis share Cam McEvoy's frustration and displeasure with the convoluted Olympic qualification rules for the 50m stroke events (50 Fly, 50 Back, 50 Breast).
They break down why the 50s of stroke get zero respect — weird time windows, priority spots eaten up by versatile swimmers, and how specificity in sprinting is punished while versatility is rewarded. Is swimming set up to kill the pure 50m specialists? The guys don't hold back.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: The Pro Swim Series is so BORING nobody sponsors it!
description: Dax & Elvis break down the newly rebranded Pro Swim Series (RIP TYR Pro Series) after the latest Austin stop. Why are meets so predictable and dull? Fast swimmers sandbagging prelims, endless C/D f...
transcript:
Dax & Elvis break down the newly rebranded Pro Swim Series (RIP TYR Pro Series) after the latest Austin stop. Why are meets so predictable and dull? Fast swimmers sandbagging prelims, endless C/D finals, zero entertainment value, and TYR shifting to CrossFit/triathlons instead. Is USA Swimming killing the sport with bad marketing and no eyeballs? We dive into sponsor ROI fails and why swimming needs to become ENTERTAINMENT. 🔥 What do you think—fixable or doomed? Drop your takes below!

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: CORRECTION: CSCAA DM'd Us – New NCAA Qualifier Rule Explained (Cinderella or Cannon Fodder?)
description: The CSCAA personally corrected us on the new NCAA automatic qualifier rule (must hit the B-cut in finals when you win the event). We discuss whether this actually creates "Cinderella stories" or ju...
transcript:
The CSCAA personally corrected us on the new NCAA automatic qualifier rule (must hit the B-cut in finals when you win the event). We discuss whether this actually creates "Cinderella stories" or just adds cannon fodder—and why our prelim auto-qualifier idea is still better.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: USA Swimming Pay Raise: $3,750/Month Isn't Enough? "Get Off Your Ass & Make Content!
description: One elite swimmer breaks it down: getting ANY stipend from USA Swimming is huge, but the recent raise to $3,750/month still feels light—yet complaining won't fix it. Prove your worth off the deck w...
transcript:
One elite swimmer breaks it down: getting ANY stipend from USA Swimming is huge, but the recent raise to $3,750/month still feels light—yet complaining won't fix it. Prove your worth off the deck with reels, podcasts, and content creation instead of begging for more. If you put in zero effort outside the pool, don't expect big bucks. Real talk on athlete value, hustle, and why personal branding matters in swimming! What do you think—is it enough or time to grind harder? 💪🏊‍♂️ #usaswimming #SwimLife #AthletePay

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Ryan Murphy is TRAINING - time to get the United States of Backstroke back on track
description: Ryan Murphy is back in the pool training for the LA 2028 Olympics after a year+ break — but is this comeback realistic? In this raw clip, we break down why his weight room focus might be holding hi...
transcript:
Ryan Murphy is back in the pool training for the LA 2028 Olympics after a year+ break — but is this comeback realistic? In this raw clip, we break down why his weight room focus might be holding him back, why getting "too big" could kill his chances at sub-52 in the 100 back, and why historic swimming comebacks (Dara Torres, Anthony Ervin, Dana Vollmer) are super rare — especially for world record holders.
We debate:

His peak WR time (51.85 relay split / 51.97 race) vs. current young guns like Will McGlothen rising fast
The mental game: Stop chasing your "old self" — build a new baseline
Pilates/yoga + technique greater than more muscle for backstroke speed
Odds: Top 8 at Trials? Top 2 for the team? Or 4th place fade?

If you're a swim nerd, this one's for you — brutal honesty on one of America's greatest backstrokers chasing home soil glory. What do you think — can he medal again?

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Billionaire Drops $100M: Every US Olympian Gets $200,000: Is It ENOUGH? 💰
description:
The Olympics are finally paying up — but is $200K per gold medal actually enough?

Dax and Elvis break down the new Olympic payout structure and debate whether swimmers are finally getting what the...
transcript:
The Olympics are finally paying up — but is $200K per gold medal actually enough?

Dax and Elvis break down the new Olympic payout structure and debate whether swimmers are finally getting what they deserve, or if this is just another half-measure.

🎯 Topics covered:
• The new Olympic bonus breakdown
• How it compares to other sports
• What this means for professional swimming
• Are swimmers STILL underpaid?

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who've seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.

New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd

#NightSwimPod #Swimming #Swimnerd

title: Tunisia OWES Coach 50K for Olympic Gold - Philippe Lucas Calls Out Federation Drama
description: French coach Philippe Lucas trained Tunisia's Ahmed Jaouadi to double World Champion last summer…and now the federation allegedly owes him €50K in bonuses. They're claiming they paid the swimmer an...
transcript:
French coach Philippe Lucas trained Tunisia's Ahmed Jaouadi to double World Champion last summer…and now the federation allegedly owes him €50K in bonuses. They're claiming they paid the swimmer and he was supposed to pass it on. Classic small-country Houdini with the money? Dax and Elvis expose the shady side of international coaching deals. Pay the man!

Who else has been stiffed by a federation? Stories in the comments.

Subscribe for the raw swimming talk the big podcasts won't touch.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd.

title: The McEvoy Method: Why Coaches REFUSE to Evolve
description:
Dax and Elvis break down Cam McEvoy's sprint training philosophy and why the swimming world keeps "discovering" what sprint coaches have known for decades.

Is the McEvoy Method really new? Why are...
transcript:
Dax and Elvis break down Cam McEvoy's sprint training philosophy and why the swimming world keeps "discovering" what sprint coaches have known for decades.

Is the McEvoy Method really new? Why are coaches afraid to try sprint-focused training? And will Elvis actually come out of retirement to test it?

🏊 Topics covered:
• Cam McEvoy's training philosophy explained
• Why sprint training keeps getting "repackaged" every 7-8 years  
• The laziness problem in coaching
• Van Matthias and the Thoroughbreds connection
• Elvis's challenge: Go 19 in the 50 free?

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who've seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.

New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming #CamMcEvoy #SprintTraining #SwimCoaching #Swimnerd

transcript:
From ankle-grabbing Lochte in a spoons-style hunt game to underwater rock-paper-scissors breath-holding battles and diabolical coach relays – Dax & Elvis share the most legendary, ruthless, and hilarious pool games ever played in swimming. You HAVE to try these (safely). Shallow water blackout is a serious thing. Do not do any crazy breath holding stuff without lots of supervision. 

Classic Night Swim chaos!

Hit subscribe for more fun swimming stories!

🌙 Night Swim Podcast

Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd.

title: UVA Women CRUSH ACCs Again! Stanford's 371-Point Beatdown + Ledecky Dominance & NCAA Power Shifts
description: Dax & Elvis break down the UVA women's swimming team's dominant 7th straight ACC Championship win — crushing Stanford by over 370 points (1410.5 to 1039) and Cal too! We dive into Katie/Kaylee Lede...
transcript:
Dax & Elvis break down the UVA women's swimming team's dominant 7th straight ACC Championship win — crushing Stanford by over 370 points (1410.5 to 1039) and Cal too! We dive into Katie/Kaylee Ledecky's insane distance swims (those sub-15:45 vibes are back), why it's not "just another Ledecky win," and the bigger picture: Is Stanford's dynasty over? What's behind UVA's steamroll era after Stanford ruled with Simone Manuel, Lia Neal, and more?
We talk cyclical nature of college swimming power (Auburn, Texas, Kentucky examples), recruiting snowball effects, fast swimmers eating scholarships, coaching impact (Bob Bowman at ASU vibes), and program identities — like Texas owning the 200 free vs UVA's hodgepodge depth (or lack thereof in some events). Plus, first-ever night swims, freestyle technique from David Marsh, and tips for coaches to challenge UVA by owning an event!


🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #swimming

title: Better Athlete vs. Better Swimmer – Which Is the Ideal Foundation?
description: Debate on whether raw athleticism (explosiveness, multi-sport background) or technical swimming "feel" makes a better recruit/building block; personal stories from both hosts, racial/physiological ...
transcript:
Debate on whether raw athleticism (explosiveness, multi-sport background) or technical swimming "feel" makes a better recruit/building block; personal stories from both hosts, racial/physiological differences (e.g., sinking legs, posterior chain), and coaching preferences.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

transcript:
Jack Kelly has gone from Brown to Texas to Ireland. His journey to LA 2028 has been an exciting one. 


🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: The NCAA Swimming Talent Gap is INSANE: Why UVA, Texas & Cal Dominate  And Most Teams Can't Catch Up
description: Dax & Elvis break down the massive talent gap in NCAA swimming that's leaving most teams in the dust. On the women's side: UVA is in a league of their own, with Texas a distant second and everyone ...
transcript:
Dax & Elvis break down the massive talent gap in NCAA swimming that's leaving most teams in the dust. On the women's side: UVA is in a league of their own, with Texas a distant second and everyone else way behind. Men's side? Texas and Cal run the show, and the rest are playing catch-up forever.
We discuss why it's so hard to close:

Resources (scholarships, facilities, money talks – especially for internationals)
Location (good weather + prestige = recruits flock to Florida, Texas, Cal, UVA)
Elite coaching & recruiting cycles (chicken-or-egg dominance)
Mentality & racer mindset (top teams recruit fearless competitors, not just fast bodies)
Real talk on building programs: Throw money at stars, pray the rest step up... or get stuck supplicating to land anyone.

Stories include: Why Hawaii could dominate but doesn't, Indian River as a feeder for internationals, full-ride demands from Caribbean families, and how track athletes go anywhere for money while swimmers chase specific coaches/programs.
Perfect for swim nerds, college recruits, parents, and fans wondering why the same teams win every year. NCAA 2026 champs approaching – UVA women projected to score 569+ points (235 ahead of #2)!

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: 18 Is the New 19?! Swimflation Is Out of Control.
description:
Are we living in the era of Swimflation?

In this episode of Night Swim, we dive into:

Why 18 might be the new 19 in the 50 free

The rise of “freshman records” in college swimming

UT’s legendary...
transcript:
Are we living in the era of Swimflation?

In this episode of Night Swim, we dive into:

Why 18 might be the new 19 in the 50 free

The rise of “freshman records” in college swimming

UT’s legendary Quadrathlon scoring system

Suits vs training vs better athletes

Why Freddy Busquet doesn’t get enough credit

And whether swimmers just needed “permission” to go faster

Is it better athletes? Better training? Tech suits? Or are times just inflated across the board?

We’re keeping it real.

Drop your take in the comments 👇
Is Swimflation real?

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

transcript:
Ilya Kharun just switched to represent the USA. Dax and Elvis have THOUGHTS.

The Canadian-born butterfly star is now competing for America. Is this good for the sport? Is it fair? What does it mean for international swimming?

🎯 Topics covered:
• The Ilya Kharun nation switch
• Rules around changing countries
• How this affects Team USA and Canada
• The ethics of nation-switching in swimming

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who've seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.

New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd

#NightSwimPod #Swimming #Swimnerd

transcript:
Van Mathias (Indiana/John Long) is quietly dominating—new Pro Swim Series records, top US times in breast, and flying under the radar! Dax & Elvis connect the dots: John Long's Queens/Team Elite/Auburn/David Marsh roots = power swimming gold. Why nobody's talking about him, mustache meta roast, and why IU might be the new breaststroke factory. Dark horse for USA teams? We need him on the pod! 🚀

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Swimmer Nutrition: Stop Starving Yourself - Eat EVERYTHING (Our Hot Take) 🍔
description: Young swimmers are malnourished AF because of crazy diets—Dax says "eat it all" especially pre-college! We roast restrictive parents, share Lochte Skittles stories, and drop gold from Jack Roach: "...
transcript:
Young swimmers are malnourished AF because of crazy diets—Dax says "eat it all" especially pre-college! We roast restrictive parents, share Lochte Skittles stories, and drop gold from Jack Roach: "Shop around the grocery store, NOT the aisles." Processed food is the enemy. Mind blown? Tell us your worst nutrition fail! 💪

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Michael Andrew's KAATSU Secret Exposed – Game-Changer or Gimmick? 🤔
description: Blood flow restriction cuffs on Michael Andrew? We dive deep into KAATSU training: how it works, the rehab origins, potential gains for swimmers, but is it worth the risk/cost? Dax questions the me...
transcript:
Blood flow restriction cuffs on Michael Andrew? We dive deep into KAATSU training: how it works, the rehab origins, potential gains for swimmers, but is it worth the risk/cost? Dax questions the mental edge—does it build real toughness or create weakness? Elite insights + savage takes. What do YOU think? Comment below! 🔥 #KAATSU #MichaelAndrew #SwimTraining #NightSwim

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: 2026 NCAA Swimming Predictions & New Qualification Rules
description: Predictions for the upcoming NCAA Championships (Texas favored to repeat), analysis of the new auto-qualification system (34 extra qualifiers, top schools like Texas losing spots), roster limits, d...
transcript:
Predictions for the upcoming NCAA Championships (Texas favored to repeat), analysis of the new auto-qualification system (34 extra qualifiers, top schools like Texas losing spots), roster limits, depth vs. one-offs, and thoughts on whether more access inspires future improvement.

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Simone Manuel Doing 7,000 Yard Practices at 29?! Bob Bowman is COOKING Her!
description: Simone Manuel admits in her SwimSwam interview: She's never done 7k practices before... until Bob Bowman! She's looking jacked and swimming solid (50 free win, close battles), but is this high-volu...
transcript:
Simone Manuel admits in her SwimSwam interview: She's never done 7k practices before... until Bob Bowman! She's looking jacked and swimming solid (50 free win, close battles), but is this high-volume madness right for a sprinter? Dax & Elvis debate: Is she exercising or training? Will she crash mid-season like many female athletes on yardage spikes? Sprint vs. IM group mismatch, inflammation risks, and why she needs to stand up for herself. Real talk for Chocolate Thunder! 💪

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Practice vs Meets: Which REALLY Matters More in Swimming?
description:
In this heated discussion, we dive deep into one of the biggest debates in competitive swimming: Does practice matter more than meets, or do meets matter more than practice?
We break down real scen...
transcript:
In this heated discussion, we dive deep into one of the biggest debates in competitive swimming: Does practice matter more than meets, or do meets matter more than practice?
We break down real scenarios:

Can crushing 51s in practice for hours make a 51 LCM 100 free "easy" at taper?
Why grinding 19.9s for two hours translates better to dropping to 19.0 than endless 51s do for sprinting.
The myth of "practice warriors" — kids who dominate practice but disappear on race day.
Why simulating meets in practice (suit-up Saturdays, race-pace Fridays) is game-changing.
One coach's story of racing EVERY meet one summer... and getting FASTER with LESS training volume.

We explore energy systems, race specificity, bringing the meet into practice (vs practice into the meet), and why true elites often underperform in practice but shine when it counts.
Whether you're a swimmer, parent, or coach, this episode will make you rethink how you train and race. Drop your thoughts below: Are you a practice warrior or a meet performer? Which side are you on — practice greater than meets or meets greater than practice?

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Michael Andrew Ditches ASU: Back to Dad's USRPT Roots in Hawaii for 2028 LA Olympics!
description: Olympic gold medalist Michael Andrew shocks the swimming world by leaving Arizona State's powerhouse sprint environment after just over a year to make a full-time return to training with his lifelo...
transcript:
Olympic gold medalist Michael Andrew shocks the swimming world by leaving Arizona State's powerhouse sprint environment after just over a year to make a full-time return to training with his lifelong coach and dad, Peter Andrew, in Hawaii—reuniting for his signature Ultra-Short Race-Pace Training (USRPT) method as he eyes a strong push toward the 2028 LA Games. After a disappointing 2024 Olympic Trials miss and a solid bounce-back at ASU, this family-rooted move blends lifestyle vibes, renewed focus, and that unbreakable father-son bond—what's next for MA? Let's break it down!

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: $1,000 for the first NCAA swimmer to do a cannonball celly + mention Night Swim
description: Dax & Elvis go OFF on why competitive swimming is too serious and needs MORE FUN! From 1980s beer carts & toga parties to modern ideas like cannonballs into sponsor bowls, touchdown dances after wi...
transcript:
Dax & Elvis go OFF on why competitive swimming is too serious and needs MORE FUN! From 1980s beer carts & toga parties to modern ideas like cannonballs into sponsor bowls, touchdown dances after wins, and breaking the "act like you've been there" rule – is it time to make swimming disrespectful and entertaining like the NFL?

They even put money on the table: $1,000 for the first NCAA swimmer to do a real celly + mention Night Swim!

No lifeguards, no filter – just raw takes on injecting personality into the sport.
Subscribe for more controversial swimming talk! 🏊‍♂️💥

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: Adam Peaty Marries & Changes Name to Adam Ramsey Peaty – British Tradition or Branding Genius?
description: Adam Peaty (now Addie Ramsey Peaty?) changes his name after marrying into the Ramsey family. Is this British royal tradition, a branding masterstroke, or just hilarious breaststroker energy? Dax & ...
transcript:
Adam Peaty (now Addie Ramsey Peaty?) changes his name after marrying into the Ramsey family. Is this British royal tradition, a branding masterstroke, or just hilarious breaststroker energy? Dax & Elvis roast, analyze, and share wild name-change stories from swimming history. Wild episode! 😂
Like + subscribe for more swimming hot takes!

🌙 Night Swim Podcast
When the pool lights go out, the real talk begins. Hosted by Dax Hill (NCAA champ, Texas Longhorns legend, high-performance technique guru @Reykli) and Elvis Burrows (Bahamian Olympian, national record holder, founder of Burrow's Best swimmer hair and skin care), Night Swim is unfiltered swim culture — NCAA battles, Olympic tea, training hacks, and pool-deck chaos. No scripts. No PR. Just two Black swimmers who’ve seen it all, saying what everyone else whispers.
New episodes weekly. Grab your goggles, hit subscribe, and dive in.
🎙️ Presented by Swimnerd. 

#NightSwimPod #Swimming

title: The Vaccine Backlash Before Tokyo
description: Dive into the controversy surrounding Olympic gold medalist Michael Andrew's bold decision to skip the COVID vaccine before the Tokyo Games—sparking backlash from teammates, media storms, and debat...
transcript:
Dive into the controversy surrounding Olympic gold medalist Michael Andrew's bold decision to skip the COVID vaccine before the Tokyo Games—sparking backlash from teammates, media storms, and debates over personal choice vs. team risk in this eye-opening look back!

#NightSwimPod #Swimming
